search_objective: Find and verify whether candidate files close the remaining Gov_Reg runtime gaps

candidates.why_considered: Strongest readable ingest/runtime implementation found. It is an active watcher/runtime module with an end-to-end ingest path, gate execution, registry writing, and evidence output.
candidates.behavior_summary: Implements a real IDPKG ingest engine: resolves file and directory identity, builds registry patch operations, applies registry writes with atomic helpers, runs gates, and emits evidence. It stops at ID/runtime scope and does not invoke analyzers, SHA256 promotion, lookup joins, or post-write generators.

conflicts_detected: Broader registry shape than current files[]-centric automation
No SHA256 to file_id promotion path
No unified column runtime or analyzer dispatch stage

risks: Scope is narrower than the unresolved Gov_Reg automation pipeline
Does not run post-write generators
Does not close the analyzer-to-patch-to-generator chain

candidates.why_considered: Name and behavior match the expected ingest/orchestrator role, and it is imported by the current pipeline runner.
candidates.behavior_summary: Runs Phase A and Phase B analyzer subprocesses and writes analysis outputs, but it does not resolve canonical IDs, mutate the registry, apply patches, run gates, or execute post-write generators. Phase C is explicitly skipped.

reads: phase analyzer scripts in the scripts directory

candidates.why_considered: Closest readable automation-level runner that chains orchestrator, transform, metadata, and patch generation steps.
candidates.behavior_summary: Builds a file_id index from registry files[], stages transformed outputs, collects run metadata, and can invoke the patch generator. It does not itself apply the patch or serve as single canonical runtime authority. REG_AUTO_PLAN.txt still treats it as incomplete.

best_candidate.reason: Best readable real orchestrator, but its authority is limited to ID/runtime ingest and does not close the full Gov_Reg analyzer-to-write-to-generator pipeline.

closure_assessment.reason: A real orchestrator exists for ID/runtime scope, and transitional automation runners exist, but there is no single canonical end-to-end Gov_Reg orchestrator that handles file-added intake through analyzers, patch application, post-write generators, evidence, and completion validation.
missing_behaviors: single file-added canonical runtime entrypoint
canonical analyzer dispatch integrated with registry mutation
SHA256 to file_id promotion before dependent writes
lookup/join resolution during derivation
post-write generator execution
final completion validation over the whole flow

candidates.why_considered: Contains the only strong readable GateRunner implementation found in-repo.
candidates.behavior_summary: Runs gates in deterministic catalog order inside the IDPKG runtime and fails the overall status when BLOCK-severity failures occur. This is real enforcement logic, but it is embedded inside the ID runtime rather than serving as one shared gate authority for all registry automation.

reads: event envelope and runtime registry state
writes: evidence JSON via the enclosing runtime

conflicts_detected: Not used as the shared gate runner for REGISTRY_AUTOMATION scripts

risks: Embedded subsystem runner, not a shared repo-level enforcement bridge

candidates.why_considered: Closest readable non-IDPKG gate bundle; defines multiple gates and writes gate evidence.
candidates.behavior_summary: Implements CompletenessGate and ValidityGate, but not a single deterministic gate runner. It assumes transition-style registry parts instead of the current files[] automation shape and leaves deeper validation TODOs.

best_candidate.reason: Only strong readable deterministic GateRunner found, but it is not the one shared enforcement entrypoint for the broader Gov_Reg runtime.

candidates.why_considered: Contains the strongest readable RegistryWriter implementation found in-repo.
candidates.behavior_summary: RegistryWriter performs controlled upserts and patch recording, reads and writes registry JSON through atomic helpers, and uses lock-backed shared utilities. It is the safest real writer found, but it is embedded in the ID runtime and is not the one accepted mutation path for all registry automation.

conflicts_detected: Not the shared writer for patch_generator/apply_patch/promotion flows
No global compare-and-swap authority across every mutation caller

risks: Safe writer exists only inside one subsystem
No evidence that other mutation callers are forced through it

candidates.why_considered: Direct patch-application CLI whose name and behavior match a mutation-authority candidate.
candidates.behavior_summary: Applies RFC-6902 patches with a dry-run option and backup creation, but writes directly after patching and does not use lock or atomic replace discipline. It is a mutation tool, not a single-writer authority.

candidates.why_considered: Closest readable patch builder/applier in the active automation path.
